{"step_by_step_thinking": "Hunter-Schreger bands are a structural feature of enamel that is formed due to the arrangement of enamel rods. Enamel is derived from the ectoderm during tooth development. Enamel spindles, enamel tufts, and enamel lamellae are all components of the enamel matrix and are therefore of ectodermal origin. Therefore, the structure that is not of ectodermal origin is Hunter-Schreger bands.", "answer_choice": "A"}
